programme.title: UFO

programme.title: The Seventies

programme.title: MoviePass, MovieCrash
programme.desc: The story of MoviePass, the company that offered cinema tickets at a discounted rate. It was a movie lover's dreaam, but in just eight years it went from being the fastest-growing subscription service since Spotify to total bankruptcy. This documentary chronicles the visionary mission of its co-founders, its early success and how it finally failed due to mismanagement and corporate greed.

programme.title: Building Outside the Lines

programme.desc: The Cordrays decide on a main kitchen entry for a tiny backhouse, salvaging the original sink and a few other antique surprises; Ashley and Michael try their hand at homemade tile, which turns out to be their favorite project yet.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Ashley and Michael let the fun and games begin as they tackle his and hers shotgun homes in a friendly competition to bring style and new life back to these two unique houses located just a couple blocks from the beach.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordrays restore an early 20th century beach bungalow for an older couple who have deep roots in Galveston; they bring the original charm of the home that had been lost in the living room, dining room and bathroom spaces.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: In renovating a very old, very unique backhouse, the Cordrays create a kitchen where there once was a porch, build an office space that used to be an attic, and find a distinct dining solution perfect for a small home.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Michael and Ashley pick up a job half done, restoring bedrooms and a kitchen for a young family in an 1886 Victorian home in the historic Stocking District of Galveston.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: Buying a newer home isn't the Cordray's norm, but adding their iconic character, style and functionality never gets old; they build a privacy fence for the deck, a herringbone design focal wall, and raise and make over the original kitchen cabinets.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordrays look to balance function with history as they tackle a living room, bathroom and dining room remodel for a young couple and their three children in their 1896 Victorian home in Galveston, Texas.

programme.title: Restoring Galveston
programme.desc: The Cordrays bring beauty, impeccable design and increased value to this renovation by adding a bedroom, vintage doors to separate the kitchen from the living space and retro tile found under the house for a one-of-a-kind backsplash.
